name: Pressed Duck with Sweet and Sour Plum Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the duck in a large kettle or Dutch oven with enough boiling water to cover. Add spices and salt, then simmer covered until tender, about 1 to 1 1/4 hours.
2. Remove the duck from the liquid and let it cool. Remove the meat from the bones and discard the skin.
3. In an 8-inch aluminum pan, sprinkle the chestnut powder evenly over the duck meat. Press the meat into the powder until it is fully coated.
4. Cover the pan and steam for 30 minutes, or until the powder has gelatinized into a thick, heavy crust. Remove from the steamer and let cool.
5. Just before serving, prepare the sweet and sour plum sauce; set aside and keep warm.
6. Warm a serving platter in a low oven.
7. Preheat oil in a deep fryer to 375°F.
8. Slice the duck into bite-size chunks and fry quickly in batches until crisp and browned. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Repeat until all the duck is cooked.
9. Serve immediately, topped with sweet and sour plum sauce and crushed toasted almonds.
